title: The Neoliberal Influence on South Africa's Early Democracy and its Shortfalls in Addressing Economic Inequality
author: Fourie

opening_move.description: Post-Apartheid South Africa has become one of the world's most unequal countries despite political liberation. The paper investigates how neoliberal economic policies (RDP, then GEAR) adopted by the ANC government contributed to deepening rather than reducing economic inequality.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: The dramatic shift from Marxist to neoliberal policies reflects the influence of global market neoliberalism and represents the ANC government's failure to address the structural economic causes of inequality.

theoretical_positioning.description: Foucauldian governmentality framework (neoliberalism as political rationality) is applied to diagnose the ANC's policy shift. The paper reads the RDP-to-GEAR transition as adoption of neoliberal political rationality, not merely economic pragmatism, and diagnoses its failure to address structural inequality.

epistemic_mode.qualitative_methods: Policy document analysis: RDP (1994), GEAR strategy (1996), ANC policy statements; secondary economic data on South African inequality (Gini coefficient, unemployment, HDI).
data_sources: World Bank/UN economic data on South Africa
epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Foucauldian governmentality analysis of policy documents; political economy of neoliberalism.
epistemic_mode.notes: The policy document analysis is constitutive: the paper's argument about neoliberal political rationality rests on close reading of RDP and GEAR as texts expressing contrasting rationalities. Economic statistics are illustrative of the outcome. Classified normative_with_qualitative_empirical (constitutive for the policy analysis, illustrative for the statistics).

case_type.case_name: Post-Apartheid South Africa's economic policy transition (RDP → GEAR, 1994–2000)

normative_stake.domain: political economy / neoliberalism / postcolonial democracy / South Africa

confidence_notes: Type 5b (constitutive critical analysis): policy document analysis of RDP and GEAR is constitutive — the argument about neoliberal political rationality depends on showing what these documents actually say and how they represent the shift. Economic statistics are illustrative of the outcome. The Foucauldian framework is imported to interpret the case.
